full_text:
- Gujarat Heatwave Alert | Changing Weather Pattern & Unseasonal Warming
Sunday Big StoryExperts warn of rising day & night temperature in Gujarat:New spell of scorching heat expected soon; changing weather pattern behind unusual warming
- Copy link
Winter had just bid farewell from Gujarat. It was the beginning of March when people were discussing one thing - look at this heat. The Meteorological Department issued a red alert... issued a yellow alert... We usually hear such words at the end of March every time, but this time severe heat was experienced at the beginning of March itself. The Meteorological Department had to issue a heatwave alert.
In the last 2-3 days, unseasonal rain during daytime in many districts of the state provided relief from severe heat but increased farmers' worries. In today's Sunday Big Story, we will be finding out why there is severe heat at the beginning of March and now unseasonal rain, and when will the new round of heat begin?
Divya Bhaskar obtained information about this from Dr. Mehul Vasani, Research Associate at Anand Agricultural University, and weather expert Paresh Goswami.
Heat on one side, unseasonal rain on the other
This time in Gujarat, summer has arrived with an aggressive mood. The 40-degree heat that usually appears in the second fortnight of March reached 42-43 degrees around March 10th this year.
March reminded of monsoon
Overcast skies, dust storms swirling everywhere, and unseasonal rain with mini cyclones created an atmosphere that felt like monsoon. In March 2015, around 5.7 mm rainfall was recorded. While in March 2023, 13.9 mm and in March 2025, 3.9 mm rainfall was recorded. Due to the unseasonal rain this March, the year 2026 has been included in the top 10 years with the highest unseasonal rainfall in March in the last 125 years.
Dust Storm Days Increased The Meteorological Department's data from 1981 to 2010 shows that the number of dust storm days has also increased in Gujarat. North Gujarat ranks first in this.
What is the reason for severe heat at the beginning of March?
Dr. Mehul Vasani, Research Associate at Anand University, told Divya Bhaskar, "Typically March, April and May are months that record the highest temperatures throughout the year, but in the last 4-5 years, except for one or two years, temperatures are being recorded at 40 degrees every year. It usually happens that after the second fortnight of March, temperature in Gujarat reaches 40 degrees Celsius, but this time it happened that from around March 10 itself, Gujarat's temperature reached 42 to 43 degrees.
Explaining the mathematics behind reaching such high temperatures since the beginning of the summer season, Dr. Vasani said, “The first reason is global warming, but other micro-level causes are equally responsible. This time, compared to usual, winter has also been very warm”.
Weather expert Paresh Goswami has predicted that there could be 2 more rounds of heatwaves this time. He said, “Normally summer lasts from February 15 to June 15. During this period, heatwave rounds occur. When the temperature goes above 42 degrees, we see it as a heatwave”.
He further added, “During one summer season, there are approximately five heatwaves. Each round typically lasts 8 to 10 days, but looking at the current situation, there could be 2 additional rounds of heatwaves this time”.
Lockdown-like situation will arise: Paresh Goswami
Paresh stated, “The next heatwave round may begin around April 3 or 4. During this, the average temperature in many areas of the state will reach up to 41 degrees. Secondly, wind speed will also play a major role. Due to higher temperatures during afternoon hours, a lockdown-like situation will be created”.
Meanwhile, Dr. Mehul Vasani explains the reason for early onset of heat saying, “When frequent Western Disturbances occur, there is good snowfall in Northwest India. This has a direct impact on Gujarat and Western India. This time, due to weak and fewer disturbances, there was 60% less rainfall than average. Because of this, the temperature in the Indian subcontinent has not cooled down as much as it should have”.
Early change in direction of north-western winds
He added, “Another reason is that the direction of north-western winds has changed early. In fact, this wind process should have continued longer but it changed in the first week of March itself. When winds coming from dry regions arrived early, instead of having some coolness, the heat came early”.
Which cities in Gujarat will see temperature rise.
While indicating which cities may experience temperature rise, Paresh said “The first round of heatwave came during March 9 to 13 this time. During this period, we saw temperatures of 41 degrees and even higher recorded at multiple places. After that the temperature dropped and on the other hand there was unseasonal rain, which spread coolness in the atmosphere”.
He added, “After the unseasonal rains, the temperature will now gradually increase but during the remaining days of March, the weather will remain normal in the average range of 36 to 38 degrees. While in the remaining isolated areas, the temperature may remain around 40 degrees”.
Temperature rise due to urbanization
When asked about rising temperatures in major cities currently, Dr. Mehul Vasani said, “The main reason behind this is urbanization because concrete structures are increasing significantly in cities. Against this, the proportion of trees is decreasing. Due to this, situation of intense heat is arising. Due to mass urbanization, temperature rise is being observed in major cities of Gujarat like Rajkot, Ahmedabad, Surat”.
If unseasonal rains had come in January-February, the heat would not have increased
If the temperature had reached 40 to 41 degrees, then how did the effect of unseasonal rains suddenly appear in Gujarat? In this regard, Dr. Vasani said “This happened due to the effect of North India. Cyclonic circulation had formed from Pakistan to Rajasthan. This caused unseasonal rains, but if 2-3 such unseasonal rains had come in January or February, perhaps the temperature would not have gone so high”.
Warm Night Proportion Increased
Paresh attributed multiple factors responsible for the rise in temperature. He stated, “In the last 3 decades, temperature has increased up to two degrees. Multiple factors are responsible for the ongoing increase in temperature. Not only India but people worldwide are troubled by this problem”.
He added, “The proportion of warm nights is also increasing due to the construction of these concrete structures. Heat will be experienced at night just like during the day. While tree cutting is increasing on one side, on the other hand, the indiscriminate use of chemical fertilizers in agriculture has also increased”.
'Rising night temperatures, a great concern'
Dr. Vasani considers the rising night temperature concerning. He said, “There is no control over the increasing maximum temperature during the day. Night temperature is very important in winter. If the night temperature increases along with the day, it can be considered more dangerous for winter crops”.
Temperature increased more in 20-25 years than in 100 years
Dr. Vasani further said, “In the 20th century such a large increase in temperature was not observed but since the beginning of the 21st century, the temperature has been increasing by approximately 0.4 to 0.5 degrees every 10 years. The temperature increase in the last 20 to 25 years has been more than what was seen in the last 100 years”.
Relief will continue till end of March
He added, “If another Western Disturbance forms like the recent one and if there are sudden weather changes in the remaining days of March and April, the temperature can be controlled. If the weather remains consistently clear, there is a possibility of temperature increase. From April 15 to May, temperatures may rise higher but until the end of March, no significant increase in temperature is expected”.
Meanwhile Paresh stated, “Scientifically speaking, there should be 16 trees per person for the temperature to normalize, otherwise the effects of these concrete jungles that are being built will only increase. As the mercury levels of heat are rising, its effects are also being felt on the human body”.
Dr. Mehul added, “Trees are being destroyed at a global level. We should plant as many trees as possible. We should also save electricity because electricity is also a major factor in changing the global climate. We should only use as much electricity as we need. We should also save water. By doing this much, the irregularity of rainfall and sudden increases in temperature can be controlled to some extent”.
